text: Hey, welcome to This is My Architecture. My name is Oreste and I'm joined by Valerio from GE Avio.
Hi Valerio, nice to have you here. Hi Oreste, thanks for having me.
Valerio, could you please tell us something about GE Avio?
Yeah, GE Avio is an excellence of Italian manufacturing. Our main business is to design and produce engine parts for both military and civil aircraft.
Okay, I know that GE Avio is moving a lot of workloads on AWS. Could you please tell us something, what are you presenting us today?
Yeah, we are here to present our solution to special expense management system.
And our employee at the end of each quarter have to manage some special expenses.
And to do that, they can open a ticket directly on our application.
This is our workload with the spikes. And the old application starts to become slow to manage this workload.
So we decided to start from scratch and build a new application in a cloud native way.
Okay, thank you. Could you please explain how the whole thing works?
Yeah, we started to with authenticating the user.
With a corporate system, OpenID.
And after the authentication, with a little help from ELB ECS Lambda function,
we are able to authorize the user.
Okay, and what happens when the user is authorized?
After the user is authorized, he can download the single page application from Docker on top on ECS.
And after having downloaded the single page application,
the application starts to collaborate with the second container on our ECS,
that is an API gateway.
Okay, cool. So what happens once the user has downloaded the application?
Where is the business logic?
Our business logic is stored here in Lambda function that are the heart of our application.
We have more or less 20 Lambda that cover all the needs of our application,
starting from creating new tickets, edit the old ones,
and manage the shift of the states of the tickets,
and also upload some attachments to an S3 bucket.
We choose two storage.
The first is DynamoDB that stores mainly metadata from the tickets.
And the second one is an S3 bucket that is used to save the attachments that are financial documents.
So we put it in a S3 bucket, encrypted at rest with KMS technology.
Oh, nice.
So what goals you achieved with this application?
We achieved a very reliable application, capable to scale at the need.
And in eight months of production, we have just one ticket not related to the architecture.
And finally, we save a lot of money.
So Valerio, thank you for having explained to me your application.
Thank you for having me.
And thank you for watching.
This is my architecture.
This is my architecture.
